_meta.purpose: Controlled vocabulary fed into the Scribe prompt and used by the validator. Keeps Claude from drifting into non-dental synonyms.
sources: ADA Glossary of Dental Clinical Terms

anatomy.mesial: Toward the midline of the dental arch
anatomy.distal: Away from the midline of the dental arch
anatomy.furcation: Area between roots of a multi-rooted tooth

conditions.caries: Tooth decay caused by bacterial demineralization
conditions.pulpitis_reversible: Inflammation of pulp that resolves with stimulus removal
conditions.pulpitis_irreversible: Symptomatic pulp inflammation requiring endodontic therapy or extraction
conditions.periapical_abscess: Localized collection of pus at root apex
conditions.gingivitis: Reversible inflammation of the gingiva without attachment loss
conditions.periodontitis: Inflammation with attachment loss and bone resorption
conditions.abrasion: Tooth wear from foreign object (toothbrush, etc.)

procedures.onlay: Indirect restoration covering one or more cusps
procedures.extraction_surgical: Requires elevation of flap or sectioning

anesthetics.septocaine: Common trade name for articaine 4% w/ epi 1:100k
